name: Augment Code

runner.notes: Benchmark separately from Class A unless setup parity is documented.

inclusion: Enterprise agentic coding platform with context and governance emphasis.
pricing_model: Commercial enterprise platform; Auggie CLI and Context Engine usage require Augment account/billing.
public_stack_signals: Official docs expose Cosmos, Auggie CLI, IDE clients, Context Engine MCP, and SDLC automations.
Context Engine supports local MCP over Auggie CLI and remote hosted indexing through the Augment GitHub App.
setup_notes: Report separately when enterprise-only setup differs from local ADE setup.

sources.notes: Enterprise agentic software development positioning and product surface claims. Refresh 2026-07-15: site live; title still 'Agentic software development at organizational scale'.
Official product docs index for Cosmos, Auggie CLI, clients, and automations.
Auggie CLI overview remains published in official docs (refresh 2026-07-15).
Context Engine positioning and large-codebase context claims.
Local vs remote Context Engine MCP architecture and indexing behavior.

research.positioning: Enterprise agentic software development platform centered on organizational context, governance, review, and SDLC workflow automation.

frontend: IDE clients for VS Code, JetBrains, Vim/Neovim
data: working directory indexed in real time for local MCP
selected repository default branches indexed through Augment GitHub App for remote MCP
commit history, codebase patterns, docs, tickets, and tribal knowledge according to official docs
unknowns: Core service implementation language, storage backend, and deployment topology are not public.
Exact enterprise governance controls available by plan need account-backed verification.

infrastructure: Local development can run Auggie CLI locally as an MCP stdio server.
Remote Context Engine uses Augment-hosted HTTP MCP and the Augment GitHub App.
Enterprise workflows include code review, ticket-to-PR, security remediation, and incident-oriented automations according to public positioning.

agent_support: Context Engine MCP clients including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Zed, GitHub Copilot, OpenCode, Kiro, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, and others
orchestration: Auggie CLI interactive and automation modes
workspace_isolation: local MCP indexes active working directory
remote MCP indexes selected repositories/default branches
not a local worktree fleet product in the same class as Orca/Kandev/Tachyon
remote_mobile: no mobile companion mapped in this pass
context_memory: external sources such as docs and tickets
integrations: enterprise SDLC systems referenced in product/docs

install_surface: optional GitHub App for remote Context Engine
parity_risks: Class B enterprise platform should not be mixed into Class A local ADE leaderboard without caveats.
Account, billing, repo indexing, and policy settings can materially affect results.
Context retrieval value may require larger multi-repo tasks than the v0.1 fixture suite.
Add a future cross-repo context task before using Augment's Context Engine as a scored differentiator.

hypotheses: Organizational Context Engine is the main differentiation against local ADEs.
Enterprise governance, code review, and SDLC integrations create a moat for large teams.
MCP exposure lets Augment's context layer augment many competing agent surfaces instead of replacing them.
evidence: Official docs describe local and remote Context Engine MCP indexing, cross-repo context, docs/tickets/tribal knowledge, and smart curation.
Official docs expose Cosmos, Auggie CLI, IDE clients, Context Engine MCP, and Augment Code Review surfaces.
unknowns: Hands-on benchmark quality depends on account access and enterprise configuration.
